  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Transmitter is installed above the process taps on liquid service, which allows gas or air bubbles to become trapped in the impulse lines and transmitter body, causing measurement errors."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate the transmitter below the process taps so that the impulse lines remain self-venting and filled with liquid.",
    "Re-route impulse piping to slope downward from the process taps to the transmitter at a minimum slope of 1:12 (approx. 8%)."
  ],
  "rationale": "For liquid service, transmitters must be mounted below the process taps to prevent gas pockets from accumulating in the impulse lines, which causes false differential pressure readings and erratic measurement. Mounting above the taps violates standard installation practices outlined in API RP 551 and manufacturer guidelines.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 (Process Measurement Instrumentation)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals (Impulse Piping / Mounting section)"
  ]
}
